remember: Students will be able to recall major events and dates related to the Partition of India.
Students will be able to identify key figures and their roles in the Partition.
Students will be able to list important causes and consequences of the Partition.
understand: Students will be able to explain the impact of colonial policies on the Partition.
Students will be able to describe the role of the Muslim League and the Indian National Congress in the Partition.
Students will be able to analyze the partition of Punjab and Bengal.
apply: Students will be able to compare and contrast the experiences of different communities during the Partition.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of the Partition on the social and economic fabric of India and Pakistan.
Students will be able to apply historical concepts to understand the ongoing relevance of the Partition.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the factors that led to the failure of the Cabinet Mission Plan.
Students will be able to evaluate the role of key individuals in shaping the outcome of the Partition.
Students will be able to assess the long-term consequences of the Partition on India and Pakistan.

ncert_objectives: To understand the impact of colonial policies on the Partition of India.
To analyze the role of the Muslim League and the Indian National Congress in the Partition.
To evaluate the consequences of the Partition on the social and economic fabric of India and Pakistan.
board_exam_objectives: To explain the main causes and consequences of the Partition of India.
To describe the events leading to the Partition, including the Cabinet Mission Plan.
To analyze the ongoing relevance of the Partition in modern India and Pakistan.
